PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Mar-22-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia appeared by zoom for and with Andre Hebert (petitioner) by zoom.

Mrs. Navia stated that three declarations were filed, that Gerald is here to support the appointment of Andre, that Gerald and his wife's declarations have already been filed and that she will file Andre's declaration to clear up dates regarding the death of Andre's mother. Mrs. Navia also stated that the former conservator (Barbara Simon) of the decedent is the one who found the will in the decedent's belongings, and that they have not received any objection from Adam. There being no objections, the Petition for Letters of Administration with Will Annexed is under submission for review of the order with the court's intent to grant the petition. The will dated July 14, 2001 is admitted to probate. Andre Simon Hebert is appointed as the Administrator of the estate with will annexed with full authority. Bond is not required. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for March 19, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed prior to the hearing date, no appearance will be required on that date. Frank Mahoney, III is appointed probate referee.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Feb-14-2023 Continued To Feb-28-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rowena Navia, via Zoom, for and with Andre Hebert, Petitioner; Adam Hebert, via Zoom, interested party.

The court advises of its concern with the scant amount of information submitted to the court regarding the holographic will and therefore, the court is not inclined to admit the will at this time and advises that additional information regarding the holographic will is to be filed. Ms. Navia advises that two declarations regarding the court's concerns have been filed. The court advises that it has not seen the referenced declarations and they will not be considered until they have been filed. At Ms. Navia's request, the court queries Adam Hebert whether he intends to file a formal objection. The court acknowledges receipt of his letter and advises him that the court is unable to consider the letter unless all parties in the matter are provided copies of the letter and that he will need to file formal objections accordingly. Adam Hebert offers brief history regarding the will. The court reminds Adam Hebert that if he intends to object to the instant petition, he will need to formally file them.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Feb-28-2023 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Avila. Not Reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
